summary:
The cranial branch of the accessory nerve innervates only the sternocleidomastoid muscle; therefore, all listed pharyngeal muscles—are supplied entirely by the vagus nerve and are spared when it is transected. Thus, any of the listed muscles can be considered spared based on lacking dependency on the cranial accessory nerve.  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to core clinical anatomic principles without adding external interpretations.) 

[End Summary]
